issue_summary: Fix interrupt handling in the `Condition` overloads of `Uninterruptibles.awaitUninterruptibly`.
javadoc_after:
/** * Invokes {@code condition.}{@link Condition#await(long, TimeUnit) await(timeout, unit)} in a way * that more conveniently supports uninterruptible waits. * *

If the underlying {@code await} call is interrupted, then {@code awaitUninterruptibly} * converts that into a spurious * wakeup. This means that resulting wait is not "uninterruptible" in the normal sense of * {@link Uninterruptibles}. Still, this method allows callers to write the standard, required loop for waiting on a {@code * Condition} but without the need to handle interruption. * * @return {@code false} if the waiting time detectably elapsed before return from the method * @since 23.6 */
